name: canopy CLI surface
description: Warren's canopy facade shells out to two cn subcommands: 'cn list --tag agent --json' (returns the {success, command, prompts:[{name,version,status,tags}]} envelope filtered to active prompts) and 'cn render <name> --json' (returns {success, command, name, version, sections:[{name,body}], resolvedFrom, frontmatter}). The global --json flag is the right one — 'cn render --format json' emits a bare object without success/command, which makes auth-vs-failure disambiguation lossy. Failed renders return {success:false, command:'render', error:<msg>} on stdout AND exit 1; the warren facade peeks for the structured error envelope before falling back to exit-code interpretation.

title: Filter agents by canopy tag 'agent', not by frontmatter
rationale: Warren SPEC §2.1 wording is 'agent: true schema tag' which reads ambiguous. Canopy supports both freeform tags (string[]) and frontmatter (Record<string, unknown>); 'cn list --tag agent --json' is the supported CLI filter and matches the example in canopy SPEC.md line 73 ('tags: ["agent", "leaf"]'). Picking tag-based filtering keeps warren aligned with the canopy CLI surface — operators use 'cn create --tag agent' to register an agent, not a frontmatter field.

content: Required sections for warren's agent definition schema: 'system' is the only required section. Optional sections from SPEC §4.2 (skills, expertise_seed, burrow_config, workflow) are consumed at run-spawn time (Phase 5) — Phase 3's schema does not require them. Canopy's per-prompt --schema rules are the right place to enforce richer structural constraints; warren only enforces the minimum needed to spawn an agent.

name: Per-agent error isolation in registry refresh
description: registry.refreshAgentRegistry collects per-prompt failures into RefreshResult.skipped instead of throwing — one bad prompt (missing 'system' section, archived between list and render, etc.) shouldn't block the other 19 from registering. Transport-layer failures (cn binary missing, canopy clone unreachable) DO abort the whole refresh because there's nothing useful to partially register if the registry is unreadable. Mirrors the burrow-client transport-error mapping pattern: per-call structured errors flow through; per-channel failures throw CanopyUnavailableError.

title: Hard-reset to origin/HEAD on every refresh, no merge
rationale: Warren's canopy clone is a cache, not a working tree. Operators edit prompts in their own checkout, push to GitHub, then refresh. A regular 'git pull' can fail with conflicts if anything stray landed in the warren-side clone (half-finished cn write, file-mode drift), so cloneOrUpdateCanopyRepo does 'git fetch --prune' + 'git reset --hard origin/<HEAD>'. Default branch is auto-detected via 'git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD' with a 'git remote set-head --auto' fallback, so most repos work without configuration.

content: Warren env vars for canopy registry are warren-namespaced AND keep CANOPY_REPO_URL un-prefixed: CANOPY_REPO_URL (the operator's library URL — required, no default), WARREN_CANOPY_DIR (local clone path, default /data/canopy-repo), WARREN_CN_BINARY (default 'cn'), WARREN_GIT_BINARY (default 'git'). CANOPY_REPO_URL is the canonical name in SPEC §10.2 and matches how the operator sets it via 'fly secrets set CANOPY_REPO_URL=...' — keep it un-prefixed even though warren-side configs are normally WARREN_*.

Canopy claude-code prompt (canopy-daf3) needs an explicit commit clause: 'commit your changes; warren handles the push, you do not need to push it yourself'. Validated 2026-05-09 (run_agpet4ev6e4a): adding the clause produced ahead_by: 1 with a real diff. Do NOT instruct the agent to push — agent-side git push fails inside the sandbox (warren-1a09: /root/.gitconfig invisible to bwrap, GITHUB_TOKEN not env-passthroughed). Warren reap is the actual push mechanism (host-side, sees /root/.gitconfig). Without the commit clause, runs silently no-op (run_a98cfx1fantf: 49 turns, ahead_by: 0, branchPushed: true).

canopy claude-code (canopy-daf3) is now the sandbox operating contract: workspace map (.canopy/agent.json, .mulch/expertise/*.jsonl, .seeds/issues.jsonl), rituals (ml prime, sd ready/close, quality gates, ml record/sd close at terminal turn), steering protocol ([STEERING] user turns are priority), expectations (no opportunistic refactors, never --no-verify, network governed by burrow_config, commit-only — warren reaps and pushes per warren-1a09). The canopy library lives at github.com/jayminwest/understory.git (renamed from my-canopy).

title: CANOPY_REPO_URL is optional; warren ships built-in agents inline
rationale: First-time install required cloning a canopy library, which forced operators into a 'fork understory, set CANOPY_REPO_URL, refresh' yak-shave just to dispatch a one-line claude-code prompt. Built-ins (claude-code, sapling) at src/registry/builtins/ seed the agents table on every server boot (and in 'warren run'); a configured canopy library still loads additively over them via upsert, so library agents override built-ins by name. Provenance flows through frontmatter.source: 'builtin'; GET /agents reads it to surface source per row. doctor / readyz canopy probes degrade to informational ok:true when CANOPY_REPO_URL is unset; POST /agents/refresh and 'warren register-agent' 400 with a friendly hint.

content: Built-in agents encode provenance via renderedJson.frontmatter.source = 'builtin'; canopy-loaded agents leave it unset (canopy doesn't emit it). readAgentSource() in src/registry/builtins/index.ts is the single reader; the HTTP /agents endpoint uses it to attach a 'source' field per row.

seedBuiltinAgents preserves existing rows: if a row already exists for a builtin name (whether seeded earlier or upserted via canopy refresh), it is skipped — never overwritten. This protects library overrides across server restarts.

warren-1a09 (sandbox push has no auth path) is resolved at the contract layer, not the auth layer: V1 ships fix A (canopy claude-code/sapling builtins instruct 'Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream' + 'Do not run git push yourself' in src/registry/builtins/{claude-code,sapling}.ts) and fix D (SPEC.md:150 §4.3 step 6 documents the agent-commits/reap-pushes contract with warren-1a09 reference and bwrap /root invisibility explanation). Fix B (relocate insteadOf rule into bwrap ro-bind set, e.g. /etc/gitconfig) and fix C (env-passthrough GITHUB_TOKEN) are deferred — only revisit when V2 needs sandbox-side push (gh pr create, fork pushes, multi-remote). Token exposure is the primary tradeoff for B/C: the rewrite URL embeds GITHUB_TOKEN in plaintext, so any agent reading /etc/gitconfig extracts it.

title: Cross-repo coordination via warren step-0 seed gates downstream warren work on burrow merge
rationale: When a warren plan step depends on a runtime contract that lives in burrow's AgentRegistry, model it as a coordination-only seed (warren-0e06 pattern): the seed body pins the contract (id, supportsResume, spawn shape, RPC commands, event kinds, envPassthrough) and links to burrow's tracking seed + plan. The warren seed stays open until burrow ships a tagged release containing the runtime AND a cross-repo PR link is recorded back into the warren seed. Closing before burrow ships unblocks downstream warren work (the pi builtin add, prepare-workspace seeding, multi-provider surface, cost columns) that would then hard-fail with NotFoundError('agent runtime not registered: pi') at dispatch — the warren->burrow runtime-id forwarding contract (mx-7352f4, mx-b0a02c, mx-c8fc41) gives no fallback. The seed acts as both a contract-spec artifact and a tombstone preventing premature closure of dependent steps.

name: pi runtime contract (warren-side expectations on burrow piRuntime)
description: Pinned via warren-0e06 / burrow-8aff cross-repo coordination. Warren forwards agent.name='pi' as the burrow runtime id (mx-7352f4) and seeds workspaces via agents:['pi'] in POST /burrows (mx-c8fc41); burrow's piRuntime must register accordingly. Contract: id='pi'; displayName='Pi'; supportsResume=true (pi --continue / --session); spawn shape 'pi --mode rpc --no-session [--no-extensions] --provider <…> --model <…>' (RPC mode, not --mode json — pl-4374 alt #2 chose RPC for the bidirectional steer/abort/get_state/get_session_stats surface that warren's spawn-per-turn flow assumes); RPC commands consumed by warren: prompt, steer, abort, get_state, get_session_stats; event kinds parseEvents must surface: agent_start, agent_end, turn_start, turn_end, message_start, message_update, message_end, tool_execution_start, tool_execution_update, tool_execution_end, queue_update, compaction_start, compaction_end, auto_retry_start, auto_retry_end, extension_error (warren-70af UI renderer expects this superset; unknown kinds fall through generic); envPassthrough widens beyond claude-code's ANTHROPIC_* + CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN set to include OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, PI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY (warren-f8c0 multi-provider depends on this — implemented at the runtime contract layer, not via docker-compose.yml, per burrow's envPassthrough escape hatch); installCheck via 'pi --version' (runVersionCheck shape). If burrow ships piRuntime with a different id (e.g. 'pi-coding-agent'), update warren's BUILTIN_AGENTS name in lockstep — the two strings must match exactly.

content: AgentDefinition.frontmatter.provider and .model are the well-known optional string fields for the multi-provider surface (warren-f8c0). They live in the open frontmatter bag — no schema rev. readProviderFrontmatter() narrows them to typed strings; withProviderOverrides() folds operator-supplied per-run overrides onto a freshly-cloned AgentDefinition before spawnRun freezes runs.rendered_agent_json. Empty/whitespace overrides are no-ops; the base agent row is untouched (override is per-run, not per-agent).

Adding a new built-in agent (alongside claude-code/sapling/pi) requires updates in five places in lockstep: (1) src/registry/builtins/<name>.ts with the AgentDefinition (mirror SAPLING_BUILTIN shape: name, version=1, sections.system + sections.burrow_config, resolvedFrom=['builtin:<name>'], frontmatter.source='builtin'); (2) src/registry/builtins/index.ts BUILTIN_AGENTS array + re-export; (3) src/registry/builtins/builtins.test.ts assertions on BUILTIN_AGENT_NAMES + readAgentSource; (4) scripts/acceptance/scenarios/13-container-smoke.ts builtin-names check; (5) src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx header + empty-state copy. Note: scenario 02-agents-refresh.ts checks 'no library-sourced agents before refresh' via filter source !== 'builtin', so it auto-extends without a code change.

PI_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/pi.ts) ships the same parity shape as SAPLING_BUILTIN (system + burrow_config sections, network='open'). The pi-specific surfaces — multi-provider override (frontmatter.provider/model), cost tracking (runs.cost_usd), .pi/skills + .pi/prompts materialization, pi-namespaced event kinds — are layered in follow-on steps of pl-4374 (warren-846b/f8c0/a7dc/70af/f1da), not in this builtin file.

title: Pi extensions deferred until canopy ships an artifact type
rationale: Pi extensions (.pi/extensions/*.ts) are TypeScript source modules with access to ExtensionAPI (registerTool, registerCommand). Encoding TS source inside a canopy section body works but is awkward — operators lose tsc/biome on the source, syntax errors only surface at run time, and the canopy diff view becomes useless. The right fix is a canopy artifact type (file-shaped, not section-shaped) that warren materializes alongside the rendered AgentDefinition. That is a canopy-side change first. V1 ships pi_skills + pi_prompts (already file-shaped + markdown via the JSONL {name, body} envelope materialized by src/runs/seed.ts) and leaves .pi/extensions/ to a follow-up plan once canopy ships artifact types. Documented in SPEC §11.K.

title: Pi has no MCP support — worldview difference, not omission
rationale: Pi explicitly has no MCP — its tool surface is built-in plus extensions only. This is a worldview difference from claude-code/sapling, not a missing feature. R-15 (MCP via canopy frontmatter, post-V1) stays scoped to claude-code and sapling and is NOT a blocker for the pi built-in. Operators wanting MCP-style external tools on pi should reach for pi's extensions story once canopy artifact types land. Documented in SPEC §11.K; ROADMAP R-15 cross-repo readiness table continues to track canopy mcp_servers frontmatter + burrow per-run credential mount for claude-code/sapling only.

title: Pi headless auth posture: API-key-only in V1; OAuth /login unsupported by design
rationale: Warren passes provider API keys into the sandbox via burrow's envPassthrough (widened beyond ANTHROPIC_*/CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN to include OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, PI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY per the warren-0e06 / mx-0db923 piRuntime contract). Pi's interactive /login flow (OAuth for Anthropic Claude Pro/Max, ChatGPT Plus, GitHub Copilot, etc.) is unsupported by design: warren is headless — no browser, no interactive TTY for the OAuth callback. A future 'bring-your-own-browser' shape for OAuth-only providers is reconsiderable in V2 if there is demand. In V1, pi is API-key-only; operators using Claude Pro/Max subscriptions should fall back to claude-code which already wires CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN via burrow-e9e7. Documented in SPEC §11.K.

content: AgentSource is a three-tier union (R-03, pl-fef5): 'builtin' (inline built-ins), 'library' (canopy library and the unset-frontmatter fallback), and 'project:<projectId>' (project-tier .canopy/ rows, stamped at render time). Read provenance with readAgentSource(renderedJson); classify coarse tier with agentSourceTier(source); extract the project id with projectIdFromAgentSource(source); construct a project source with makeProjectAgentSource(projectId) (rejects empty). Refresh helpers overlay frontmatter.source via stampAgentSource(agent, source) — original AgentDefinition is not mutated. readAgentSource collapses 'project:' with empty suffix back to 'library' so a malformed row doesn't masquerade as the project tier.

CanopyClient (src/registry/canopy.ts, pl-fef5 step 4 / warren-a8b0) now stores {cnBinary, cwd} directly instead of a CanopyRegistryConfig. Two static factories: CanopyClient.forLibrary({config, spawn?, timeoutMs?}) wires the library clone (cwd = config.localDir) and CanopyClient.forProjectPath({projectPath, cnBinary?, spawn?, timeoutMs?}) wires the project tier (cwd = projectPath, cnBinary defaults to 'cn'). 'cn' resolves '.canopy/' from cwd, so forProjectPath takes the project root not '<projectPath>/.canopy'. New callers should reach for a factory rather than calling 'new CanopyClient' with raw {cnBinary, cwd}.

refreshProjectAgents (src/registry/refresh.ts, pl-fef5 step 5 / warren-91bd) is the project-tier counterpart to refreshAgentRegistry: takes a CanopyClient.forProjectPath(...), lists+renders prompts from <projectPath>/.canopy/, calls stampAgentSource with makeProjectAgentSource(projectId) to overlay frontmatter.source='project:<id>', and upserts at projectId scope so global rows are untouched. Per-agent failures collected into RefreshSkipped (same shape as library refresh); transport-level cn errors throw (the all-projects loop in step 6 catches them). Prune is always-on (not opt-in like the library), because .canopy/ is the authoritative source for the project tier — listForProject(projectId) is diffed against the seen-set and missing rows deleted via repo.delete(name, {projectId}). Return shape is {projectId, registered, skipped, removed} matching the planned POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh response.

projectCanopyClient(deps, projectPath) (src/server/handlers.ts, pl-fef5 step 6 / warren-7777) is the handler-side factory wrapping CanopyClient.forProjectPath. cnBinary defaults to deps.canopyConfig?.cnBinary ?? 'cn' so project-tier refresh works even when CANOPY_REPO_URL is unset; spawn = deps.spawn ?? defaultSpawn so tests can stub cn invocations without touching PATH.

On-disk rendered cache for project-tier agents (warren-44e3, follow-up to R-03/pl-fef5): refreshProjectAgents (src/registry/refresh.ts) accepts optional projectPath + cacheWriter. When projectPath is set, init() once seeds <projectPath>/.canopy/.rendered/.gitignore (single line '*\n' — self-ignoring so the cache stays out of project commits), write() per registered agent dumps the stamped AgentDefinition (frontmatter.source = 'project:<id>' included) as pretty-printed JSON to .canopy/.rendered/<name>.json, prune() per removed row rm-fs the matching file. defaultRenderedCacheWriter validates names against /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$/ — unsafe names skip the fs write (the agents-table row is still the authoritative cache). handlers.ts (refreshAgents loop and refreshProjectAgentsHandler) now pass projectPath: project.localPath. Tests that create projects with a localPath must use a real writable tmpdir, not a sentinel path like /data/projects/x/y.

name: cross-tier compose fallback for project-tier roles
description: When cn render fails for a project-tier role because extends/mixins reference a name outside the project's .canopy/, refreshProjectAgents (src/registry/refresh.ts) falls back to a warren-side composer (src/registry/compose.ts) that fetches the raw prompt via cn show --json, walks parents through a resolver that tries project tier first (cn show) then global tier (agents.get), and merges sections + frontmatter using canopy's own algorithm (parent first, mixins left-to-right, focal last; empty-body removes; later overrides earlier). Source stamp tracks the LEAF tier (project:<id>); resolvedFrom preserves the full chain.

title: Walk past name shadows by passing visited chain to the parent resolver
rationale: When a project role A extends a name X that ALSO has a project-tier shadow, naively resolving X to the project shadow loops (the shadow itself extends X). The composer passes the current visited chain to the resolver; the production resolver in refresh.ts skips the project tier when the parent name already appears in visited, falling through to global. This matches the seed's open question: per-project wins for the leaf, but parent resolution walks past name shadows.

content: cn --json show <name> returns the un-resolved prompt envelope {success, command:'show', prompt:{...}} including extends/mixins/sections/frontmatter; not-found exits non-zero with a structured {success:false, error: "Prompt 'X' not found"} (single quotes) or 'Prompt "X" not found' (cn render — double quotes). CanopyClient.showAgent collapses the not-found envelope to null so the compose resolver can fall through tiers without string-matching elsewhere.

BRAINSTORM_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/brainstorm.ts, pl-0344 step 6 / warren-3de8) is the first interactive built-in agent — registered in BUILTIN_AGENTS alongside claude-code/sapling/pi. It's a read-only scout: warren's burrow_config only forwards [sandbox].network (src/runs/burrow_config.ts), so the no-writes/no-dispatch contract is enforced in the system prompt, not the sandbox profile. Sets network = "open" for web-fetch scouting and tags frontmatter with ["agent", "interactive"] so the UI can filter interactive-mode pickers. Prompt drives the user toward the four Plot intent fields (goal / non_goals / constraints / success_criteria) — fixture tests in builtins.test.ts pin those strings so a casual edit can't silently widen the role.

PLANNER_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/planner.ts, warren-543d / pl-0344 step 7) is the second interactive built-in agent, pairing with brainstorm. Tagged 'agent' + 'interactive' in frontmatter (same as brainstorm) so the UI interactive-run picker can filter without parsing the system prompt. burrow_config sets [sandbox].network = 'open' for web scouting; richer per-tool ACLs aren't wired through src/runs/burrow_config.ts yet, so the path-scoped write contract ('.plot/' and '.seeds/' only, no source files, no dispatch, no git writes) is enforced in the system prompt. Registered in BUILTIN_AGENTS in src/registry/builtins/index.ts.

BRAINSTORM_BUILTIN's Formalize-parser contract (src/registry/builtins/brainstorm.ts, warren-d22e / pl-0344 step 8): the system prompt advertises a field-marker format the formalize parser (src/plots/formalize.ts MARKER_RE) anchors. Shape is **goal**: <one-line>, **non_goals**: / -<item>, **constraints**: / -<item>, **success_criteria**: / -<item>. Case-insensitive; bold/heading/dash separators tolerated. The singular goal field is overwritten by the most recent claim, list fields accumulate deduplicated. Editing the prompt's marker examples without updating the regex (or vice versa) will break the brainstorm \u2192 formalize roundtrip silently \u2014 the builtins fixture test (src/registry/builtins/builtins.test.ts) pins the prompt strings; src/plots/formalize.test.ts pins the regex behaviour.

readRuntimeId(agent) (src/registry/schema.ts, warren-ebca) is the seam src/runs/spawn.ts uses to resolve the burrow runtime id for dispatch: prefers frontmatter.runtime (string, non-empty) and falls back to agent.name. System-prompt-only canopy agents whose name does NOT match a BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES entry (brainstorm/planner — and any future library agent that layers on top of claude-code/sapling/pi) MUST set frontmatter.runtime, or burrow rejects dispatch with 'agent <name> is not registered' and warren reaps as never_started. claude-code/sapling/pi builtins keep name=runtime convention and need no stamp.

description: seedBuiltinAgents (src/registry/builtins/index.ts:82-86) early-returns when a row with the builtin's name already exists. On upgrades that change a builtin's renderedJson (e.g. adding a frontmatter field like warren-ebca's frontmatter.runtime), the stale v_prev row sticks around — the new code reads cached agents from the DB via readCachedAgent, so the source-side fix is dead text on every existing install. Symptom on warren-deployed: brainstorm/planner dispatch still hit 'agent X is not registered' after v0.5.1 boot even though src/registry/builtins/{brainstorm,planner}.ts had been patched and shipped.
resolution: Hand-patch via UPDATE agents SET rendered_json = jsonb_set(rendered_json, '{frontmatter,runtime}', '"claude-code"'::jsonb, true) WHERE name IN ('brainstorm','planner'). Permanent fix tracked as warren-ced0: re-upsert when existing.frontmatter.source === 'builtin', preserving library/project tier overrides.

content: seedBuiltinAgents re-upserts pre-existing built-in rows if their content/frontmatter has drifted from code, while preserving overrides from library and project tiers

Nightwatch and bugwatch built-in agent prompts (src/registry/builtins/{nightwatch,bugwatch}.ts) instruct the LLM to set step-level labels on every plan step it submits: nightwatch sets labels: ["nightwatch"] and bugwatch sets labels: ["bugwatch"] per step. This extends the parent-seed --labels patrol,nightwatch (or bugwatch) tag (sd create step) to all spawned children, so plan-run filtering by agent label works across the whole patrol tree. Prompt-only change — relies on the step-level labels field landing in sd plan submit. warren-ccbe.

pr-fixer builtin (warren-05ea) is the first source-editing builtin without auto_plan_run frontmatter — the CI poller dispatches it directly rather than reap's plan-run path. runtime='pi', network='open'. System prompt forbids opening a new PR (warren pushes to the existing PR branch) and forbids skip/delete of failing tests as a workaround.

LEVERET_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts, warren-fdd9) is the first 'conversation'-tagged built-in and the first with runtime='pi-chat' — readRuntimeId forwards frontmatter.runtime as a free string so no KNOWN_RUNTIME_IDS change is needed. It ships its single pi extension via the sections.pi_extensions JSONL {name,body} line (seeded to .pi/extensions/propose_intent.ts by buildSeedFiles). The propose_intent tool is a pure proposal carrier: execute() returns the field-scoped intent patch ({goal,non_goals,constraints,success_criteria}) on result.details and writes nothing — warren correlates it host-side via tool_execution_end toolCallId into intent_edited(actor=leveret). The def grants read-leaning tools only (read/grep/find/ls/bash, NO edit/write) so the send-off PR is plot-state-only by construction.

title: Default runtime is pi, not name-fallback (warren-16f8)
rationale: Pi is the preferred multi-provider runtime: cost streams in-band (warren-17a4), supports the unified provider matrix, and is what most dogfood runs use. claude-code should be opt-in, not the implicit default.

content: frontmatter.tools (warren-8dee) is read by readToolsFrontmatter in src/registry/schema.ts: {allow?,deny?,noBuiltins?,noTools?} -> pi --tools/--exclude-tools/--no-builtin-tools/--no-tools. allow/deny coerce string[] OR comma/whitespace string; bool flags tolerate 'true'/'false' strings (warren-5f07 trap). parseRenderedAgent + validateAgentDefinition reject malformed shapes and freeze the normalized policy onto frontmatter so it forwards to burrow via composeBurrowMetadata (same seam as provider/model). Only pi consumes it in V1; paired with burrow buildPiArgv (SPEC 11.K).

warren-3db0: the 'healer' built-in (src/registry/builtins/healer.ts) is a dedicated pi-runtime, Opus-tier closed-loop incident-repair agent dispatched by POST /alerts/heal. Unlike pr-fixer it opens a fresh branch+PR (alert = production breakage, not an in-flight PR). Registered in BUILTIN_AGENTS; builtins.test.ts iterates all builtins so it conforms automatically.
